content:
Of course. Here are the memory aids for mapping a conceptual design (ER model) into a logical design (relational model).

```json
{
  "mnemonics": ["E-R-S: Entities become Relations, Relationships become either Foreign Keys or a new Relation (table), and handle Subtypes.", "FAT-S: Flatten Attributes, Assign Types, Set Primary/Foreign Keys."],
  "keyPoints": ["Strong entities become tables with their own primary key.", "Weak entities become tables and include the primary key of their owner as a foreign key.", "Many-to-Many relationships become a new relation (table) with the primary keys of the related entities.", "One-to-Many relationships are mapped by adding a foreign key on the 'Many' side table.", "Subtypes can be mapped using a 'all-in-one' table, multiple tables, or a hybrid approach."],
  "commonMistakes": ["Forgetting to create a new table for a many-to-many relationship and trying to use only foreign keys.", ["Creating a separate table for a one-to-one relationship instead of simply merging the entities or using a foreign key in one of the tables.", "Incorrectly assigning a primary key to a weak entity without including the foreign key from the owner entity.", "Not properly handling null values when mapping specialization (subtypes) into a single table."]
}
```
